notes_md:
**Bao gồm** means *to include* — to have something as part of a whole. It’s used when listing parts that make up something bigger.

It often appears in formal or semi-formal contexts (plans, lists, services, reports). In casual speech, people often shorten it to **gồm** — same meaning, more natural in conversation.


It focuses on what is inside the set, not the total amount. A more formal synonym is **bao hàm**, but that’s used more in academic writing.
exampleSentences: Danh sách này bao gồm tất cả các thành viên trong nhóm.
Món ăn này bao gồm thịt gà, rau và nước sốt đặc biệt.
Chuyến du lịch sẽ bao gồm vé máy bay và khách sạn cho mọi người.
notes:
Bao gồm means to include — to have something as part of a whole. It’s used when listing parts that make up something bigger.

It often appears in formal or semi-formal contexts (plans, lists, services, reports). In casual speech, people often shorten it to gồm — same meaning, more natural in conversation.

It focuses on what is inside the set, not the total amount. A more formal synonym is bao hàm, but that’s used more in academic writing.

definitions.en: to contain or comprise something as part of a whole
definitions.es: contener o comprender algo como parte de un todo
definitions.vi: chứa hoặc bao gồm một cái gì đó như một phần của tổng thể

example.en: Your shopping cart includes three items.
example.vi: Giỏ hàng của bạn bao gồm ba sản phẩm.
example.es: Tu carrito de compras incluye tres artículos.

translation.en: This book includes a lot of information.

exampleSentencesTranslated: This list will include all members in the group.
This dish will include chicken, vegetables, and a special sauce.
The trip will include plane tickets and hotel for everyone.
